title: Palantir's first quarter revenue surged 85% as AI platform demand drives forecast upgrades
text: Palantir Technologies delivered first-quarter results that significantly exceeded expectations, with revenue reaching $1.63 billion, an 85% year-over-year increase that beat Wall Street consensus of $1.54 billion. The standout performance came from US commercial revenue, which jumped 133% year-over-year. This segment represents Palantir's push to establish its AI Platform (AIP) as the standard infrastructure for enterprise AI adoption. Total contract value in US commercial reached $1.18 billion, up 45% annually, with remaining deal value climbing 112% to $4.92 billion. The company reported 206 deals exceeding $1 million in total contract value during the quarter, compared to 180 previously. This reflects Palantir's land and expand strategy, acquiring enterprise customers, embedding the platform, then scaling contracts. Federal business remained robust, with US government revenue at $687 million, up 84% year-over-year. This addressed investor concerns about potential weakness in government procurement spending. Both revenue segments grew at comparable rates, eliminating one persistent objection to the stock. Palantir raised its full-year revenue guidance to $7.65-$7.66 billion from the prior $7.18-$7.20 billion range, effectively adding $470 million in annual expectations. Chief executive Alex Karp signaled continued confidence, stating expectations for US commercial revenue to grow at 100% year-over-year in 2027. At approximately $144 per share, the stock trades at roughly 100 times forward earnings, leaving minimal room for execution missteps.
